top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: Hospitality News Middle East's Post - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: The Saudi Red Sea Authority has appointed Dr Maryam Ficociello as CEO, effective March 22, 2026, marking a new leadership phase.
top_posts.draft_comment: New authorities typically struggle with regulatory credibility in their first three to five years. What determines survival versus acceleration is whether the CEO treats governance infrastructure as a product, not a formality. Dr Ficociello's background in resilience and compliance suggests she understands this distinction at scale.

top_posts.author: Hotelier Middle East
top_posts.title: Maryam Ficociello appointed CEO of Saudi Red Sea Authority
top_posts.preview: Maryam Ali Ficociello as chief executive officer, effective March 22, 2026. The authority said the appointment supports its mandate to develop
top_posts.draft_comment: Coastal tourism governance in a region this ambitious requires a delivery mindset, not just a regulatory one. From a PMO lens, the real test will be whether she can simultaneously build compliance frameworks and investor confidence without one compromising the other.

top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: Saudi Red Sea Authority names Dr Maryam Ali Ficociello as CEO
top_posts.preview: #Industry | The Saudi Red Sea Authority has appointed Dr Maryam Ali Ficociello as CEO, effective March 22, 2026. Bringing more than 20 years
top_posts.draft_comment: Governance and sustainability leadership at the executive level is still an underrepresented combination in the GCC executive pipeline. Her appointment signals that the Authority is treating environmental protection as a governance discipline, not a public relations exercise. The next 18 months will confirm whether the structure matches the ambition.

top_posts.title: #ai #robotics #aigovernance | AI for Good - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Our #AI and #Robotics Programme Officer Guillem Martínez Roura is heading to Riyadh to speak at the 3rd Global AI ... Artificial Intelligence
top_posts.draft_comment: The convergence of AI governance across marine, coastal and digital ecosystems is a gap most PMOs have not yet mapped. Having a structured governance layer before technology deployment is what separates lasting programs from expensive pilots. Worth watching how the roundtable frames this for regional adoption.

top_posts.title: Santosh Kotnis' Post - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Santosh Kotnis. CEO | Building SpringUp | Driving AI-Enabled IT Services, BOT-Based Global Delivery & Scalable GCC ... Industry 4.0 : Disruptive
top_posts.draft_comment: Cross-institutional leadership forums like this are where the most transferable PMO lessons surface. The intersection of emerging leaders and seasoned practitioners in a structured setting tends to produce the sharpest frameworks. Looking forward to what gets built from this.
